classDescription: An inspiring spellcaster, performer, and lorekeeper.
roleIdentity: Support-controller hybrid with broad utility and magical flexibility.

bard-spellcasting.name: Spellcasting
bard-spellcasting.summary: You gain access to spell slots, a spellcasting ability, and a growing library of magical options.
bard-spellcasting.description:
Spellcasting is the rules engine that turns this class into a caster. It should clearly define your spell ability, slot progression, preparation or known-spell rules, and where those spells are surfaced in the sheet.

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

bard-bardic-inspiration-d6.name: Bardic Inspiration (d6)
bard-bardic-inspiration-d6.summary: Grant an ally a die they can spend later to swing a key roll in your party’s favor.
bard-bardic-inspiration-d6.description:
As a bonus action, you inspire a creature so it can add the Bardic Inspiration die to an important future roll. This is one of the bard’s core support tools and should feel visible, easy to spend, and easy to track.

In play, use it before decisive attacks, saves, or ability checks instead of hoarding it too long. The sheet should make the die size, remaining uses, and refresh timing obvious.

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

Refresh depends on level; later bard progression improves how often this comes back.

Practical effect: Creates a flexible support resource that helps the party pass important rolls.
bard-bardic-inspiration-d6.recovery: Refresh depends on level; later bard progression improves how often this comes back.
bard-bardic-inspiration-d6.effect: Creates a flexible support resource that helps the party pass important rolls.

bard-jack-of-all-trades.name: Jack of All Trades
bard-jack-of-all-trades.summary: You are broadly competent even outside your best skills.
bard-jack-of-all-trades.description:
Jack of All Trades raises the bard floor on many checks, making the class feel smart, flexible, and rarely useless outside its primary proficiencies. This should influence derived skill math across the sheet, not just live as flavor text.

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

bard-song-of-rest-d6.name: Song of Rest (d6)
bard-song-of-rest-d6.summary: Your party gets better value from downtime healing during rests.
bard-song-of-rest-d6.description:
Song of Rest improves short-rest recovery for the group. It is not an action button you spam in combat; it is a rest-phase quality boost that should be visible when the table is reviewing recovery options.

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

bard-bard-college-subclass.name: Bard College (Subclass)
bard-bard-college-subclass.summary: Choose your college and unlock a stronger identity for the rest of the build.
bard-bard-college-subclass.description: This is the subclass gate for the bard. Once chosen, your college should drive later feature cards, action buttons, resource hooks, and playstyle expectations instead of sitting as a hidden background choice.

bard-expertise.name: Expertise
bard-expertise.summary: You push chosen skills into true specialty territory.
bard-expertise.description:
Expertise doubles down on a few signature proficiencies so your character becomes exceptional instead of merely trained. In the sheet, the chosen skills should clearly jump above ordinary proficiency math.

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

bard-ability-score-improvement.name: Ability Score Improvement
bard-ability-score-improvement.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
bard-ability-score-improvement.description:
Ability Score Improvement is one of the biggest character-shaping progression points. It can either raise the numbers your class depends on most or open a feat that changes what the character can do.

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

bard-cantrips-known-3.name: Cantrips Known: 3
bard-cantrips-known-3.summary: Your known cantrip count is now 3.
bard-cantrips-known-3.description:
This progression point raises your total known cantrips to 3, giving you more at-will magical options in and out of combat.

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

bard-font-of-inspiration.name: Font of Inspiration
bard-font-of-inspiration.summary: Your inspiration engine is now much easier to keep online.
bard-font-of-inspiration.description:
This progression point changes the cadence of Bardic Inspiration recovery. It matters because it decides whether the bard can treat inspiration as a premium panic button or as a regular combat rhythm tool.

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

bard-bardic-inspiration-d8.description:

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

Refresh depends on level; later bard progression improves how often this comes back.

Practical effect: Creates a flexible support resource that helps the party pass important rolls.
bard-bardic-inspiration-d8.recovery: Refresh depends on level; later bard progression improves how often this comes back.
bard-bardic-inspiration-d8.effect: Creates a flexible support resource that helps the party pass important rolls.

bard-subclass-feature.name: Subclass Feature
bard-subclass-feature.summary: This level opens or advances your subclass path, adding themed powers unique to your chosen specialization.
bard-subclass-feature.description:
A subclass unlock means the base class starts branching into a more specific identity. The sheet should surface the actual subclass feature rows beside this progression point so the player can tell what changed.

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

bard-countercharm.name: Countercharm
bard-countercharm.summary: Use performance or force of presence to protect allies against mental disruption.
bard-countercharm.description:
Countercharm is a defensive support action for situations where fear, charm, or similar control effects are threatening the party. It is a situational feature, but when the right fight appears it should feel like a real answer, not dead text.

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

bard-magical-secrets.name: Magical Secrets
bard-magical-secrets.summary: You learn powerful off-list magic, widening what your bard can solve.
bard-magical-secrets.description:
Magical Secrets is one of the bard’s biggest identity spikes. It lets you poach spells that radically change your support, control, or burst toolkit. The sheet should make those extra spell choices feel first-class, not like a hidden exception.

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

bard-ability-score-improvement-2.name: Ability Score Improvement
bard-ability-score-improvement-2.summary: Improve key ability scores or take a feat to reshape the build.
bard-ability-score-improvement-2.description:

bard-epic-boon.name: Epic Boon
bard-epic-boon.summary: Gain an endgame reward that pushes the build into truly legendary territory.
bard-epic-boon.description:
Epic Boons are late-game progression spikes that add a high-power identity capstone beyond ordinary class scaling.

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

bard-superior-inspiration.name: Superior Inspiration
bard-superior-inspiration.summary: Even when running dry, you are less likely to start an important scene empty-handed.
bard-superior-inspiration.description:
Superior Inspiration is an endgame reliability feature. It exists so the bard’s signature support economy feels online more often at the start of high-level encounters.

This shines most when it helps the party pass an important check, save, or attack.

progressionSummary.summary: Jack of All Trades, Song of Rest (d6).
Expertise, Magical Secrets, Bardic Inspiration (d10), Cantrips Known: 4.

buildTips.primaryReason: powers your spells and social skills
buildTips.secondaryReason: improves your armor class, initiative, and finesse/ranged attacks
buildTips.avoidDumpReason: low HP and weak Concentration saves can make a Bard feel fragile
